title: Snake Bite
summary: Many snake bites can be dangerous or fatal. Quick, calm action and immediate medical attention are essential for survival.
steps: Move away from the snake immediately — do not attempt to catch or kill it.
Keep the person calm and still to slow down venom spread through the body.
Remove all jewelry and tight clothing from the bitten area as swelling may occur.
Immobilize the bitten limb at or below heart level.
Mark the edge of the swelling with a pen, noting the time.
Transport the person to the nearest hospital as soon as possible.
If possible, note the snake's appearance (color, size, pattern) to help identify it.
Keep the person lying down during transportation.
what_to_do: Keep the person and the bitten limb still
Get to a hospital as quickly as possible
Immobilize and keep the bitten limb below heart level
Note the time of the bite and any symptoms
Keep the person calm to slow the spread of venom
what_not_to_do: Do NOT suck the venom out with your mouth
Do NOT cut or incise the bite wound
Do NOT apply a tourniquet or ice
Do NOT give caffeine or alcohol
Do NOT allow the person to walk if avoidable
Do NOT try to capture or kill the snake
when_to_call_ambulance: Immediately after any suspected venomous snake bite
Pain, swelling, or discoloration at the bite site
Nausea, vomiting, or dizziness after the bite
